description: Multi-turn conversations testing panel data analysis workflows

conversations.description: FE vs RE selection with Hausman test
conversations.dataset_context: grunfeld dataset: firm, year, inv, value, capital (panel: firm=entity, year=time)

turns.user_prompt: I have panel data on firm investment. Start with a fixed effects model
Now estimate a random effects model for comparison
Run a Hausman test to decide between FE and RE
Based on the test, estimate the appropriate model with clustered standard errors

conversations.dataset_context: trade dataset: export_value, distance, gdp_origin, gdp_dest, origin, dest, year

turns.user_prompt: Estimate a gravity model of trade with country fixed effects
I need to control for both origin and destination country effects simultaneously
Add year fixed effects as well - so three-way fixed effects

conversations.dataset_context: employment dataset: emp, wage, output, firm, year

turns.user_prompt: Estimate a fixed effects model of employment on wages and output
Test for serial correlation in the residuals using the Breusch-Godfrey test
Run the Driscoll-Kraay estimator for panel-robust standard errors

conversations.dataset_context: firm_dynamics dataset: profit, investment, assets, firm, year

turns.user_prompt: I suspect there's persistence in profits. Include a lagged dependent variable
Use the Arellano-Bond GMM estimator to handle endogeneity
Check the AR(2) test results for serial correlation in the differenced residuals

conversations.dataset_context: state_data: gdp, population, spending, state, year

turns.user_prompt: Estimate state GDP as a function of population and spending with state fixed effects
Add time fixed effects to control for common shocks
Use GLS to account for heteroskedasticity and serial correlation
